title: Gerunds and infinitives
explanation:
Gerunds and infinitives

Some verbs are followed by a gerund (-ing): enjoy, avoid, consider. Others are followed by an infinitive with to: decide, hope, plan, want. Some can take both with a change of meaning.

questions.feedback: Enjoy is followed by a gerund.
Suggest is followed by a gerund.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: I hope ____ from you soon. | *to hear | hearing | hear
She finished ____ the kitchen before dinner. | *cleaning | to clean | clean
questions.feedback: Finish is followed by a gerund.

questions.text: We agreed ____ outside the station. | *to meet | meeting | meet
Do you mind ____ the window? | *opening | to open | open
I would like [*to learn|learning|learn] Italian next year.
He kept [*talking|to talk|talk] during the film.
They want [*to move|moving|move] to a bigger house.

questions.feedback: Deny is followed by a gerund.
